SEARCH
Package com.example.eventlotterysystem.UI.fragments

Class GeoRequirementFragment

java.lang.Object
androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
com.example.eventlotterysystem.UI.fragments.GeoRequirementFragment
All Implemented Interfaces:
android.content.ComponentCallbacks, android.view.View.OnCreateContextMenuListener, androidx.activity.result.ActivityResultCaller, androidx.lifecycle.HasDefaultViewModelProviderFactory, androidx.lifecycle.LifecycleOwner, androidx.lifecycle.ViewModelStoreOwner, androidx.savedstate.SavedStateRegistryOwner
public class GeoRequirementFragment extends androidx.fragment.app.Fragment
Fragment that allows an organizer to enable or disable the geolocation requirement for their event. US-02.02.03: As an organizer I want to enable or disable the geolocation requirement for my event. Acceptance criteria covered: - Toggle to enable/disable (defaults to disabled). - When enabled, organizer picks "location included" or "location excluded", sets a center point and radius. - Settings are persisted to Firebase. - When enabled, entrants currently on the waiting list who do not meet the requirement are removed.

    • evaluateRequirement

      public static boolean evaluateRequirement(Double entrantLat, Double entrantLng, double centerLat, double centerLng, double radiusKm, String mode)
      Decide whether an entrant at (entrantLat, entrantLng) satisfies the geolocation requirement.

      If the entrant has no stored location their coordinates are null; they are treated as not meeting the requirement (safe default).

      Parameters:
      entrantLat - latitude stored in the entrant's waiting-list entry
      entrantLng - longitude stored in the entrant's waiting-list entry
      centerLat - center latitude of the requirement zone
      centerLng - center longitude of the requirement zone
      radiusKm - radius of the zone in kilometres
      mode - "included" (entrant must be inside) or "excluded" (entrant must be outside)
      Returns:
      true if the entrant satisfies the requirement
